title: 2026 Oscars Best Supporting Actor Rankings
text: Delroy Lindo – Sinners ranks fifth. Lindo finally received his first Oscar nomination for playing Delta Slim. The reviewer notes that while one of the biggest, most welcome surprises in this year's nominations, the character lacks the weight of other nominees in this category. Jacob Elordi – Frankenstein ranks fourth. Elordi portrays The Creature in Guillermo del Toro's adaptation. Despite having only nine weeks to prepare after Andrew Garfield's departure and enduring a 10-hour daily makeup process, he delivered a sympathetic interpretation of this classic character. Benicio del Toro – One Battle After Another ranks third. Del Toro plays Sensei Sergio St. Carlos in Paul Thomas Anderson's film. The reviewer describes him as such an unusual character with so many angles and highlights that in terms of fun characters in this category, he's absolutely the winner. Sean Penn – One Battle After Another ranks second. Penn's portrayal of Col. Steven J. Lockjaw showcases his range—creating both a terrifying threat and a ridiculous character simultaneously. The performance is described as particularly relevant to contemporary politics. Stellan Skarsgård – Sentimental Value ranks first. Skarsgård tops the ranking as filmmaker Gustav Borg. The reviewer argues his nomination represents arguably a case of category fraud, as he functions as the lead in Joachim Trier's drama, and deserves recognition for an exceptionally layered performance.
